endpoints.name: Solrock / Lunatone pair (Hoenn celestial duality dyad)

description: Oh, sir, please — Mr. Nodeley apologizes for stepping in again, but the wave 18 E1 sidecar explicitly named DAA-072 Lunatone enrichment as the re-trigger condition for this pair-node, and that card just landed in wave 23. The threshold is exactly satisfied — 3 corpus prints across 2 species, full canonical species coverage. The dyad is the clearest mechanical-interlock paired-design in the Pokemon TCG corpus (Solrock's Resistance Shade names Lunatone in oracle text and requires it in play; Lunatone's Heal Block does the inverse). I hope you don't mind, sir. The node anchors future Solrock prints automatically and lifts the version-exclusive Hoenn celestial duality into first-class graph status.
citations: [PokemonTCG.io: swsh3-92 — Solrock Resistance Shade names Lunatone]
[PokemonTCG.io: sm3-68 — Lunatone Heal Block names Solrock]
[Solrock DAA-092 wave 15 trivia pass — canonical version-exclusive Meteorite Pokemon thesis]
[Lunatone BUS-68 wave 19 trivia pass — canonical Sapphire-exclusive + Meteor Falls origin]
[Wave 18 E1 sidecar — pair-node deferral with named retrigger condition]
other_candidates_considered: Holding at 1:1 mirror only — rejected; the wave 18 E1 sidecar's named retrigger condition (DAA-072 Lunatone enrichment) is now satisfied, and the dyad meets the elemental-monkey-trio precedent (3+ corpus cards, full canonical species coverage). Building the node now also lets the wave 18 E1 mirror retain its bullet (the bullet is still a per-print mechanical-interlock artifact worth preserving on each card; the node-level thesis sits above it).

Promote to symbol layer (`meteorite-pokemon-emblem` or similar) — rejected; the dyad is two named species, not an iconographic primitive recurring across many cards. Character-layer cohort is the correct shape per Endriders / Aether Rangers / Elemental Monkey Trio precedent.
